content.problemStatement:
JJ has an array AAA of length NNN. He can perform the following operation on AAA:


Select any LLL, RRR (1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N1 \le L \le R \le N1‚â§L‚â§R‚â§N) and for each iii such that L‚â§i‚â§RL \le i \le RL‚â§i‚â§R, set Ai:=Ai+1A_i := A_i + 1Ai‚Äã:=Ai‚Äã+1.
JJ wants to make AAA palindromic. Find the **minimum** number of moves to do so.

Note: An array is called a palindrome if it reads the same backwards and forwards, for e.g. [1,3,3,1][1, 3, 3, 1][1,3,3,1] and [6,2,6][6, 2, 6][6,2,6] are palindromic.


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT ‚Äî the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains an integer NNN ‚Äî the size of the array AAA.
The second line of each test case contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \dots, A_NA1‚Äã,A2‚Äã,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äã denoting the array AAA.


Output Format
For each test case, output the minimum number of moves to make AAA palindromic.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚ãÖ1053 \cdot 10^53‚ãÖ105.

Explanation:
Test Case 1: We can perform the following operations:

[2,6,4,3,4,1‚Äæ]‚ÜíL=3,R=6[2,6,5,4,5,2][2, 6, \underline{4, 3, 4, 1}] \xrightarrow{L = 3, R = 6} [2, 6, 5, 4, 5, 2][2,6,4,3,4,1‚Äã]L=3,R=6‚Äã[2,6,5,4,5,2]
[2,6,5,4,5‚Äæ,2]‚ÜíL=4,R=5[2,6,5,5,6,2][2, 6, 5, \underline{4, 5}, 2] \xrightarrow{L = 4, R = 5} [2, 6, 5, 5, 6, 2][2,6,5,4,5‚Äã,2]L=4,R=5‚Äã[2,6,5,5,6,2]

Test Case 2: We perform the operation on (L=1,R=1)(L = 1, R = 1)(L=1,R=1) 999 times after which array becomes [10,10][10, 10][10,10].
Test Case 3: AAA is already a palindrome.
